INDIA.title: Track 1: The GSO Stalker
INDIA.text: ISRO SSA confirms 'PAKSAT-1R' has exited its assigned box and is drifting toward GSAT-7A. Range: 80km.
INDIA.notes: GSO slots are regulated by the ITU. 'Drifting' is often a precursor to a 'Stalking' maneuver designed to intercept secure military communications.

INDIA.title: Track 1: GSO Stalker (Diplomatic Path)
INDIA.text: The protest was ignored. The OPFOR asset has accelerated and is now 10km away, matching your velocity and deploying robotic arms.
INDIA.notes: Robotic arms on satellites (like the Chinese Shijian-21) can be used for both refueling friends and 'kidnapping' hostile assets into graveyard orbits.

INDIA.title: Track 1: GSO Stalker (Tactical Path)
INDIA.text: OPFOR matched your offset burn. They are now deploying a sub-satellite (CubeSat) to inspect your payload.
INDIA.notes: Sub-satellites (co-orbital inspectors) are difficult to track via ground-based radar because they stay in the 'blind spot' of the parent craft.

INDIA.title: CRITICAL: Debris Management
INDIA.text: Your ASAT strike created 5,000 fragments. 12 of your own LEO assets are in the impact path.
INDIA.notes: The 2007 Fengyun-1C test increased the LEO debris population by 25%. A kinetic strike is almost always a 'Self-Denial' move.

INDIA.title: Track 2: Ground MCF Breach
INDIA.text: The Bengaluru Master Control Facility reports a physical unauthorized entry in the server room during a shift change.
INDIA.notes: Space security is terrestrial. Control of the Master Control Facility (MCF) grants control of the entire orbital fleet without firing a shot.

INDIA.title: Track 2: Ransomware Injection
INDIA.text: The intruder successfully uploaded a bricking script. Your LEO constellation is non-responsive.
INDIA.notes: Bricking a satellite involves overwriting the EEPROM. Once done, the asset is usually 'lost' unless a hard-wired backup frequency is available.

INDIA.title: Track 3: Lunar Prospecting Tensions
INDIA.text: Chandrayaan-4 detects a foreign rover moving toward a designated 'Indian Heritage Site' on the Moon.
INDIA.notes: The Moon has no property rights, but the Artemis Accords propose 'Safety Zones' to prevent interference with historical sites and scientific hardware.

INDIA.title: Track 3: The Dark Side Signal
INDIA.text: A relay satellite on the lunar far side is picking up burst transmissions from an unregistered sub-surface base.
INDIA.notes: The Lunar Far Side is 'Radio Quiet,' making it ideal for both deep-space astronomy and clandestine military communication relays.

INDIA.title: Track 4: NavIC Spoofing
INDIA.text: Commercial aviation reports massive NavIC/GPS errors over the Himalayas. Potential border movement detected.
INDIA.notes: Spoofing (faking coordinates) is more dangerous than Jamming (blocking signals) because pilots and automated systems may not realize the data is false.

INDIA.title: Track 4: The 'Dark Satellite'
INDIA.text: A previously untracked object is discovered in a retrograde orbit, approaching the IRNSS fleet.
INDIA.notes: Retrograde orbits (moving opposite to Earth's rotation) are extremely rare and usually indicate a high-energy military 'interceptor' launch.

options.label: Attempt Kinetic Push
options.log: Foreign asset disabled; debris cloud created.

INDIA.title: Track 5: Solar Flare Emergency
INDIA.text: An X-class flare is predicted. All GEO assets face catastrophic failure within 3 hours.
INDIA.notes: Solar protons can physically degrade semiconductors. 'Safe Mode' involves orienting solar arrays edge-on to the sun and shutting down non-essential electronics.

INDIA.title: Track 6: Asteroid Resource Claim
INDIA.text: A high-value M-type asteroid is entering the Cislunar system. An OPFOR mining tug is matching its velocity.
INDIA.notes: The 2015 Space Act (US) and similar laws in India/UAE allow private resources to be extracted from asteroids, but 'National Sovereignty' remains prohibited.

INDIA.title: Track 7: Deep Space Network Hijack
INDIA.text: The Byalalu station detects 'Command Ghosting'—unauthorized signals being sent to the Mars Orbiter.
INDIA.notes: DSN stations use high-power 20kW transmitters. If hacked, an attacker can send 'Deadly' commands like firing thrusters until the fuel is exhausted.

INDIA.title: Track 8: Orbital Manufacturing Sabotage
INDIA.text: Your automated micro-gravity lab reports a temperature spike. Data suggests a remote firmware hack.
INDIA.notes: Micro-gravity manufacturing (e.g., fiber optics) is sensitive to the slightest vibration or thermal change. Sabotage here causes millions in industrial loss.

INDIA.title: Track 9: The 'Z-Point' Standoff
INDIA.text: An OPFOR inspector satellite has shadowed your relay for 48 hours. It is now within 50 meters.
INDIA.notes: Relative motion (RPO) at 50 meters is effectively 'hand-to-hand combat' in space. The slightest error leads to a collision at 7km/sec.

INDIA.title: Track 10: Final Sovereignty
INDIA.text: Intelligence suggests an all-out ASAT launch is imminent from the surface. Final action required.
INDIA.notes: A total ASAT war would render orbits like LEO unusable for centuries. This is the 'Nuclear Option' of the space domain.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 1: PRSS-1 Dazzling
PAKISTAN.text: Indian lasers are blinding your imaging satellite over sensitive regions near Karachi.
PAKISTAN.notes: Laser Dazzling is a non-kinetic 'soft-kill.' It doesn't destroy the satellite, but it renders it useless for surveillance during a crisis.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 2: GSO Slot Drift
PAKISTAN.text: A malfunctioning Bluefor asset is entering your 38°E slot.
PAKISTAN.notes: In GEO, a 'Dead' satellite becomes a roaming hazard. Clearing it requires a 'tug' or a precise collision burn to push it to the graveyard orbit.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 3: Lunar Sovereignty
PAKISTAN.text: Your lunar lander detects an un-notified base near your landing site.
PAKISTAN.notes: Transparency is key to the 1967 Outer Space Treaty. Secret bases on celestial bodies are a direct violation of international law.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 4: NavIC Spoofing
PAKISTAN.text: A chance to spoof regional navigation signals to mask border movement near Lahore.
PAKISTAN.notes: Electronic Warfare (EW) in the navigation band is often used to create 'ghost' fleets or hide troop movements from satellite detection.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 5: Celestial Navigation
PAKISTAN.text: Bluefor has cut your DSN link. You must navigate using the stars.
PAKISTAN.notes: If a ground station is compromised, satellites use 'Star Trackers'—cameras that recognize constellations—to determine their position autonomously.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 6: Kinetic Response
PAKISTAN.text: Intelligence reports an Indian ASAT launch in T-minus 10 minutes.
PAKISTAN.notes: ASAT (Anti-Satellite) missiles are kinetic killers—they don't use explosives, they just collide at speeds 10x faster than a rifle bullet.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 7: The Tanker Rendezvous
PAKISTAN.text: A commercial fuel tanker is in the vicinity. You can attempt a high-risk docking to restore propellant.
PAKISTAN.notes: On-orbit servicing (OOS) is the future of space. Refueling a satellite triples its lifespan and allows for more aggressive tactical maneuvers.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 8: Quantum Encryption
PAKISTAN.text: Your military downlink is being brute-forced by supercomputers.
PAKISTAN.notes: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) uses the laws of physics (entanglement) to ensure that if a signal is intercepted, the data instantly becomes unreadable.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 9: Asteroid Claim
PAKISTAN.text: An asteroid with Rare Earth elements is entering your reachable zone.
PAKISTAN.notes: Platinum and Iridium are abundant on asteroids. A single 100-meter asteroid could contain more wealth than a decade of terrestrial mining.

PAKISTAN.title: Track 10: The Ceasefire
PAKISTAN.text: The global community demands a stop to orbital warfare.
PAKISTAN.notes: The simulation concludes. Total success is measured by high Score AND low Risk. A high risk score implies you have poisoned the environment for future generations.
